response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong individual contributor with 20+ years of software development experience and genuine exposure to developer tooling (CI infrastructure, test runners, MCP generators), but the role is explicitly a people management position requiring demonstrated experience leading direct reports, conducting performance reviews, and driving organizational change. None of these appear anywhere in his resume. His 'Staff' titles and founder role reflect technical seniority and self-direction, not organizational leadership. His workshop facilitation and Selenium course creation show mentorship instincts, but that does not substitute for 2+ years of formal people management. Without evidence of managing engineers, he is not a viable candidate for this role as written.
strengths: Broad and deep technical background spanning 20+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and systems — well above the 8-year minimum
Genuine developer tooling experience: built CI/CD infrastructure at Altom, authored Fluent-Asserts and Trial (a D-lang test runner), and contributes MCP tooling at re:cinq
Strong knowledge-sharing and teaching track record via workshops, conference talks, and multi-day courses — relevant to a team-development mandate
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no direct reports, no performance conversations, no hiring or firing, no org-chart ownership across any role
No indication of strategic roadmap ownership or cross-team alignment at any organizational level — all impact is individual or project-scoped
No mobile-platform ecosystem or large-scale build/release infrastructure experience; the Apple TV work at WatchUp is isolated and dated (2015–2016)

key_factors.factor: No organizational leadership or roadmap ownership
